arts.ttl: Chelsea to sign USA's Girma for world record fee
arts.sum: USA defender Naomi Girma agrees terms to join Chelsea on a long-term deal for a world record transfer.

arts.ttl: Chelsea break $1m barrier to sign USA defender Girma in world-record deal
arts.sum: San Diego player agrees personal terms with WSL club Fee tops previous record of $860,000 for Kundananji The United States defender Naomi Girma is set to have a medical at Chelsea this week after agreeing personal terms to join from San Diego Wave for a world-record fee. Chelsea will pay $1.1m (£890,000), the first women’s football transfer fee to exceed a million US dollars. The Guardian understands Chelsea have agreed a long-term contract with Girma and she is set to travel to London for a medical with Sonia Bompastor’s side, who are seven points clear at the top of the Women’s Super League at the halfway stage. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: USMNT international Lennard Maloney leaves Heidenheim for Mainz
arts.sum: The 25-year-old arrives at the MEWA Arena having spent two and a half successful seasons at Heidenheim, where he played an integral role in Frank Schmidt's Bundesliga promotion push of 2022/23. Maloney becomes Mainz's first signing of the January transfer window and he arrives to strengthen an 05ers team who are pushing hard for a European finish this term.

arts.ttl: Chelsea ‘agree’ world record transfer fee for USA star
arts.sum: Chelsea Women are set to bolster their already impressive squad after reportedly agreeing a world record transfer fee for Naomi Girma. The USWNT defender is on the verge of moving to west London after the Blues “agreed to terms” on a $1.1 million (£890,000) deal with NWSL outfit San Diego Wave FC, The Athletic reports. […] The post Chelsea ‘agree’ world record transfer fee for USA star first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Bad news for Christian Pulisic ahead of crucial Champions League game
arts.sum: During his 18 months in Italy Pulisic has largely been deployed on the right wing, albeit with the freedom to drift inside and influence the game. Head coach Sergio Conceicao has confirmed that Pulisic is still struggling with the knock and is very unlikely to play against Girona.

arts.ttl: Conceiçao: “Pulisic Is Back, But I Wouldn’t Want to Risk Him”
arts.sum: AC Milan's head coach Sergio Conceiçao addressed the media in a press conference ahead of the Champions League clash against Girona, providing insights into the team's preparation and mindset. The post Conceiçao: “Pulisic Is Back, But I Wouldn’t Want to Risk Him” appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: USMNT Transfer notebook: Gio Reyna's Borussia Dortmund conundrum, Juventus' Tim Weah linked with Everton among others
